title: Grade Review: 3rd Semester of Physics Gradschool
body: Don't often comment, but I just finished my third semester in Undergraduate Physics. The main thing I can say is that the jump from U1 to U2 is insane. Coming out of high-school, you're probably one of the smartest students in the school if you decided to pursue physics, in a mathematical/logical sense. U1 is fine, but U2 really makes you feel like you're a complete idiot. Midterms are brutal. Finals are meh. But the relief you feel after completing everything is insane and it just leaves you craving to learn more. Personally, I thought I was doomed after midterms, but things started to click by the end so I ended up being very happy with how I did. Hope everyone enjoys their break :)!
author: @string433

title: Grade Review: 3rd Semester of Physics Gradschool
body: I took 5 major classes in statistics and math, and got straight A's in all of them and it actually was the first time I got straight A's since middle school
author: @connordemorest6750

title: Grade Review: 3rd Semester of Physics Gradschool
body:
Congrats! I got two A’s and an A+ this quarter and I’m stoked.

Advice for physics and math based classes:

Before exams, do any practice problems as if it were the actual test (no notes or other resources) and explain everything you do. When you’re done, see what you got wrong and what you didn’t feel super confident about. Study those subjects, do homework problems related to them, and once you feel confident, do the practice problems closed-note again. Repeat this until you know everything so well it becomes boring, or you run out of time.
author: @jaidenboucher0

title: Grade Review: 3rd Semester of Physics Gradschool
body: These videos make my day. I love them! Have a great rest of your 2019 Andrew. My advice (from a graduate student in maths perspective), one B isn't the end of the world. Two B's isn't the end of the world. Life goes on, and someday, when you are finished with school you will realize those B's weren't the end of the world.
author: @austynhughes134

title: Grade Review: 3rd Semester of Physics Gradschool
body:
I just finished the first term of part iii maths (where I'm doing theoretical physics). Before that I did an undergraduate in physics so I have plenty of advice I could give by now, mostly from my own mistakes! I'm going to limit this to two main points.

First of all, try and keep on top of work as you go. While cramming for the final exam is possible, it'll be much easier if you spend the time going over your notes as the term goes on. I found writing up neat notes after the class is a good way of forcing yourself to do this.

Secondly, and even more importantly, don't compare yourselves to other students and try not to be intimidated by them. This especially holds for when you first start and everyone has dreams of being the next Feynman. Worrying about how much better other students are than you is not going to help anything. In my experience it normally turns out that the cocky students overestimate their own abilities anyway, and won't actually put in the effort to do well in the final exam.
author: @Airsofter4692

title: Grade Review: 3rd Semester of Physics Gradschool
body: Just finished my 5th semester of undergraduate Aerospace Engineering, at Embry Riddle Aeronautical University, things have definitely gotten harder going through my junior year, but very facinating studying Aerodynamics, supersonics, and space mechanics!

title: Grade Review: 3rd Semester of Physics Gradschool
body:
Congratulations for your straight A's, that's amazing. I just finished my finals today, it's my first semester. I took:
English
Philosophy of science
Modern physics (special relativity and intro to quantum mechanics)
Geometry an Analysis lll
Civilisation studies program
I didn't do very well in my finals and I am very shocked, as I really understood everything and solved every exercise I could get my hand on. But well, I think I can still do better next semester hopefully. I don't want to mess up my future as I want to do theoretical physics which I think needs very high grades to get into a top masters program. I'd like to say to prospective students to never leave anything to the day before the deadline or the exam if it can be done earlier, NEVER. Also, don't count on your classes to teach you. Always spend as much time at the library reading anything that you can understand in which you will or will not take a course. That's what I did and I was actually able to easily and rapidly solve a junior year exam that most of the students who took that class (1 year older than me) found difficult and very long. Always learn as much as you can, whether alone or with someone.
author: @gady-manuelalaoui6243

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: Devil's Hwy. (US 491) is called "Devil's Hwy.", not because it's a dangerous road, but because it used to be designated as US 666.  In 2003, the route number was changed to US 491 mainly because too many people were stealing the "US 666" signs.
author: @YellowZeppelin93

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: It may be not a great state to live in but I gotta say it’s one of the most underrated states when it comes to natural beauty. It’s got some majestic mountains!

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: Native New Mexican here. My ancestors came here from Texas over a 100 years ago to ranch and stayed. I’ve lived in San Diego and LA but glad to be home again because the best part of this state is you can drive for miles and miles without seeing one city after another. It’s wide open spaces everywhere and most of that is open to the public. I don’t worry about people moving here and changing it because that hasn’t happened since NM became a state in 1912. 😂

And he said he has never being threatened from other people in his most of life there, although he sometimes hears a gunshot nearby downtown.

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: There’s no perfect state, I’ve lived in 7 states throughout my life, but when I moved up to northern NM to Los Alamos in 1975 from Chicago area I fell in love with this state. Friendly people that are laid-back, multi-cultures, beautiful blue skies, mountains, desert areas, lots of open land, nice weather - noting too harsh. I like that this state isn’t over populated and hope it stays that way!!!
author: @sandymartinez5434

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body:
New Mexico was my favorite place
to work wildfire. The summer weather
is wonderful. Pretty much zero humidity.
No mosquitoes, few flies. Hot days, yes,
but your sweat evaporates immediately
so you stay comfortable, cool even cold
nights perfect for sleeping.
Best food anywhere in the US. Natural beauty
everywhere. Biggest elk I've ever seen. Trout.
Turkey. Mountain lions. Silver, turquoise, art,
architecture, history. Most importantly, nice, nice, friendly people.
author: @joeharris3878

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body:
Grew up in NM, and came back for a short time in my early 20s, but the lack of decent jobs or even decent paying jobs, was the single worst part of living there.
I miss the culture, the people, the weather, the whole feeling of the state, but if you can’t make a living, then it makes it hard to stay.

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: I had never been to NM until October of 2024. The roads from Roswell to Ruidoso were new and fantastic. Although the people in Roswell were a little odd, I only had one bad experience in the week I was in NM. Ruidoso was incredible. Beautiful mountains.

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: I live in southern Colorado. I go on vacation in New Mexico several times a year. Easier to visit than to move there.
author: @chuckwilson2301

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: I was traveling there recently. Someone described it as the Mississippi of the west. Poor and undereducated was their implication. I found the natural scenery phenomenal. The fact that much of the state is above 5,000 feet makes it cold in winter and pleasant in summer. Low housing prices were in evidence too. All this combined sets the state up for and influx of people. The crime likely isn’t bad in the smaller towns which is where the retirees will head. I’d go there in a heartbeat but I’m happy with my life as it is and I’m not likely to change at age 70+.
author: @robertt9270

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: I live in Truth or Consequences, NM. It's pretty laid back. I can spot the Milky Way on my front porch.
author: @mattwales2734

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: I grew up in Seattle in the 1970s. My family is from New Mexico and Southern Colorado. You DON'T want people to "discover" your state because all it will do is push out the middle class. Seattle in the 1970s was very blue collar, but losing population to the suburbs. After 1990, the tech jobs came, and most of us who lived here got pushed out. Lucky for me that my parents bought their house cheap in 1966 and is now worth $$$$. I sold it and moved to the so called ritzy Eastside of Seattle. I am blessed, but I know many who are not😢
author: @swtv1754

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: New Mexico is not for everyone but I love it I love the fact that is not as populated as other states you can feel it on the air I think is the cleanest state of all but like I said is not for everyone
author: @marioromo5926

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: Speaking of the high crime rate, I remember when I lived there in the early to mid 2000's, hearing that the city asked the show "COPS" to quit filming in and around Albuquerque because it was hurting the tourism they counted on a few times per year!! They even had the local news stop showing violent content during the International Balloon Fiesta at the beginning of October every year because they didn't want to scare the tourists away. At one point, I wouldn't even watch the local news anymore because it was too awful and depressing!!! It's such a shame too because the culture and atmosphere is so beautiful there!!!!! It's rich in art, food, and cultural events year-round if you're into Southwest Native American and Hispanic cultural arts and food!!! And the sky is so spectacular as the sun rises and sets during the rainy season between late July through October, it will quite literally take your breath away!!!
author: @springfauna1465

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: Happy to add my voice to those thanking you for discouraging people from moving to NM and turning it into another Arizona. I’ve lived in Santa Fe for eleven years and have no air conditioner because the temps on some high desert summer nights can drop into the fifties. Yes, we’re rural, we’re poor, we’re proudly Hispanic and Native American and like not being like every green-front lawn boring suburban state in America. And thanks for not mentioning the food. Oh, man, the southwestern cuisine. I mean — sorry — the food is terrible here. Just terrible!
author: @Griffinmc

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: I've lived in Santa Fe for 20 years and love it. Glad most of the people move to Arizona, they can have them!
author: @richardyoung4616

title: 10 Shocking Truths Why People Won't Move to New Mexico
body: Have you been to New Mexico?  It is beautiful state especially the northern sections.  Amazing mountains and some first class snow skiing in Taos area.  Three ski resorts withing 30 miles including Albuquerque.  Food and Culture and churches alone are amazing.  Santa Fe, Taos, Angel Fire and so many smaller towns with that are lovely.  Of course you can find crappy things to say about every state.  Summers in Arizona are far more brutal that NM.  Most of NM is at an elevation of 5000 or higher.  And, or course if you ask young people they will say there's nothing to do.  Kids say that all the time in California,  I have property in No NM and my stress level disappears when I visit.  I will probably retire in NM.  Cost of living is far below California.
author: @darrellsephus5378

title: COLLEGE MOVE IN DAY VLOG | NMSU | PIÑON HALL | Anayancy Campos
body: I never been to NMSU (Las Cruces), but I've been to UNM (Albuquerque) and NM Tech (Socorro).  We live in Shiprock, NM.  What made you choose NMSU over other schools?  Are you from New Mexico or Texas?  What is your major?  I think you have now been at NMSU for at least 2 years.  How is it?  Do you like it?  Do you have an internship?  Would you recommend NMSU?  My daughter will graduate from San Juan College High School in Farmington, New Mexico, in May 2023 with a high school diploma and an Associates Degree in Engineering;  when she does, she might go there.  The way I was told by her counselor is that she already has a scholarship to go to UNM.  But I really don't know where she will go to a university.  I am thinking in California or New Mexico.
author: @harrisonlapahie3483

title: COLLEGE MOVE IN DAY VLOG | NMSU | PIÑON HALL | Anayancy Campos
body:
Hi dear, please am prince I got admission at NMSU Master's in public Administration Fall 2021 but I deferred it due to not getting my passport on time for my I-20 form to spring 2022.
Please I have been following u for a while on YouTube to no much about the school, can you be of help to me by indicating me more about the school or things I should no? Please

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More
body: Thanks for giving folks a personalized glimpse of UNM- it was a pleasure to host you and for taking the time to get to know us!
author: @nicoletami8993

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More
body: I want to piggyback on your scholarships portion (as someone who works at the OneStop). If you apply before December 1st and start that next fall, you will have WAY MORE scholarship opportunities and will have way more state aid opportunities!!
author: @ameliamcgraw

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More
body: I enjoyed hearing your impressions of UNM and seeing some of my favorite spots.  I was an out of state student with an Amigo Scholarship, class of ‘92. I loved my time as a Lobo.  My kid is there now, also with an Amigo Scholarship.   And I agree, UNM is a great diverse campus where everyone can find their passion.
author: @lisahopp1112

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More
body: Hi, I’m a high school student in the U.K. and this video was great. I visited UNM last year as my father was born in Albuquerque, and i thought the campus was great. Are there any other scholarships similar to the amigos for other universities in the US as I would love to keep my options for next year open. Cheers
author: @Djfocybdjdio

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More
body: It's time for me to attend a University and unfortunately this is my only option. Not excited or looking forward to going here but it is what it is. I feel like people also generally look down on this state and people from it and feel like UNM is essentially a community college. Oh well.
author: @O_G

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More
body: Sadly I don't have a 3.0+ and I'm not doing the SAT or ACT. I'll let my college hours just transfer.
author: @Lonelyeco

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More
body: A campus surrounded by crime, homelessness, and run down neighborhoods. Not to mention obnoxious sports fans who act like their teams are top notch when they are not. All in all, it’s not all that great academically or otherwise.
author: @nmelkhunter1

title: NEW MEXICO STATE UNIVERSITY CAMPUS TOUR!!! (D1 UNIVERSITY)
body: Hi! I’m a new prospective student looking at transferring to the uni from GCU for conservation ecology! I’ve heard mostly amazing things about the university, and the conservation ecology program head actually got back to me via email the other day! I am going to subscribe to your channel too because if I wind up going to the uni, I would 100% want to follow the university sports teams as I love sports too! I hope you have a great rest of your day and tysm for this video!
author: @NVSG_Official
